items.title: Aerospace and Defense Stocks to Watch: The Biggest US Contractors by Revenue
items.author: Frank Holmes
items.description: The article ranks the top 10 largest U.S. defense contractors by revenue, highlighting their key capabilities and growth prospects in the aerospace and defense industry amid rising global tensions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amentum is a leading provider of engineering and technical services, specializing in supporting government missions with $5.7 billion in defense-related revenue.

Booz Allen Hamilton is a leading provider of technology and consulting services for the Department of Defense, leveraging advanced capabilities in AI and cybersecurity to address complex defense challenges.
Leidos specializes in IT services and solutions, providing support for mission-critical systems for the Department of Defense and other federal agencies, with $11.1 billion in defense revenue.

Huntington Ingalls Industries is a leader in shipbuilding and defense technologies, generating $11.4 billion in defense-related revenue as the sole builder of U.S. Navy aircraft carriers and a key supplier of nuclear-powered submarines.
L3Harris Technologies is known for its advanced communication systems, tactical radios and next-generation jamming technology, with $15.6 billion in defense-related revenue.
Boeing's defense segment contributed $32.7 billion in revenue, complementing its commercial aerospace business, despite challenges in its commercial segment.
Boeing's defense segment contributed $32.7 billion in revenue, complementing its commercial aerospace business, despite challenges in its commercial segment.

General Dynamics' diverse portfolio spans combat vehicles, shipbuilding and IT services, generating $33.7 billion in defense revenue, and its profitability is expected to rise through improved margins.

Northrop Grumman is at the forefront of aerospace and defense innovation, with $35.2 billion in defense revenue, known for its contributions to nuclear modernization, the B-21 Raider stealth bomber and space systems.

RTX specializes in missile systems, integrated defense solutions and advanced sensors, with $40.6 billion in defense revenue, and its strategic divestitures demonstrate its commitment to delivering shareholder value.

Lockheed Martin stands as a giant in the defense sector, with a whopping $64.7 billion in defense revenue, known for its iconic programs like the F-35 Lightning II fighter jet and advanced hypersonic weapons, and its robust backlog and commitment to cutting-edge technology make it a no-brainer for defense investors.
